Scenario.title: Water Infrastructure Disruption via Phishing and Data Manipulation
Scenario.overview: Water and Wastewater Systems Simple A sophisticated attack targeting critical water infrastructure begins with spearphishing emails containing malicious payloads. The attacker exploits unpatched vulnerabilities in public-facing applications to execute arbitrary code, then manipulates operational data to disrupt treatment processes.
Scenario.attack_surface: AVAILABLE_SURFACES: Internet-facing assets, Application Log, OS Process
Scenario.attack_vector: AVAILABLE_VECTORS: Delivery Method, Application Log Content, Data Manipulation

stage.name: Initial Access
stage.description: The attacker sends spearphishing emails to facility staff, embedding malicious attachments or links that compromise credentials or deliver malware to internet-facing systems.

stage.name: Execution
stage.description: Leveraging unpatched vulnerabilities in control system interfaces, the attacker injects malicious code through application logs to execute arbitrary commands on the targeted infrastructure.

stage.name: Impact
stage.description: The attacker alters sensor readings and process control parameters in real-time, causing operational disruptions such as incorrect chemical dosing or equipment shutdowns.

Scenario.summary: A targeted attack compromises water treatment systems through phishing, exploits vulnerable interfaces, and manipulates operational data to disrupt critical infrastructure functions.
